rulings.Case Title: BENEDETTO VS GRELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment And Compel Execution And Service Of Acknowledgment Of Satisfaction Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 16, 2007, LINE 3 DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER E. GRELL IND DBA LAW OFFICE OF CHRISTO'S Notice And Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment And Compel Execution And Service Of Acknowledgment Of Satisfaction Of Judgment IS DENIED. THERE HAS BEEN NO APPLICATION TO RENEW THE JUDGMENT AND SO THERE IS NO RENEWAL JUDGMENT OF FILE. IT ALSO APPEARS TO BE THE CASE THAT THE AMOUNT OF THE RENEWAL JUDGMENT REFERRED TO BY THE PARTIES IS IN ERROR, DUE APPARENTLY TO THE FAILURE TO FILE TWO MOTIONS FOR COSTS, ON APPEAL AFTER JUDGMENT. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: VERNON FARQUHAR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 16, 2007, LINE 4, DEFENDANT CHAMPION INDUSTRIAL CONTRACTORS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEB OF PROOF. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BASED UPON THE DECLARATIONS OF ARTS AND FARQUHAR. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRATNED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTIONS FOR STRICT LIABILITY, FALSE REPRESENTATION, AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE COURT HAS CONSIDERED DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE FARQUHAR AND ARTS DECLARATIONS. ALTHOUGH THE ISSUE IS NOT FREE FROM DOUBT, IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION THAT D'AMICO DOES NOT BAR THE IDENTIFICATION OF CHAMPION AS HANSEN. PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY REGARDING HANSEN WAS NOT CONCLUSIVE. THE IDENTIFICATION IS FURTHER SUPPORTED BY ARTS. FARQUHAR DOES HAVE A FOUNDATION FOR HIS STATEMENTS REGARDING ASBESTOS INSERTS. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: RUFUS RENARD BROWN VS. BRENT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 16, 2007, LINE 19 PLAINTIFF RUFUS BROWN'S (Amended) Notice Of Motion To File Second Amended Complaint. GIVEN THE POLICY IN FAVOR OF ALLOWING AMENDMENTS AND THE FACT THAT THESE NEW CLAIMS DO NOT REALLY CHANGE THE ESSENCE OF THE DISPUTE BETWEEN THE PARTIES, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THIS DEPARTMENT DOES NOT HAVE THE AUTHORITY TO CHANGE THE TRIAL DATE. THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO MAKE THE REQUEST IN DEPT. 206. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE MORALES et al VS. CLUB ONE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Special Mtn And Special Mtn To Strike
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 16, 2007, LINE 8 PLAINTIFF MICHELLE MORALES Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike is Granted. From the face of the cross-complaint, it is self-evident that it is based solely on Ms. Morales' report of a sexual assault by defendant/cross-complainant. The decision in Siam v. Kizilbash (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 1563, 1569-1570 establishes that Michele Morales statement is protected activity under CCP section 425.16. In response, defendant/cross-complainant addresses only statute of limitations issues. Although defendant/cross-complainant is correct as to causes of action for interference with contract, and negligence, he has failed to present any evidence demonstrating an ability to prevail on the merits, let alone addressed the fact that the challenged conduct is protected, in whole or part, by Civil Code section 47(b) and (c). =(302/PJM/X-MW)

rulings.Case Title: COUNTY OF ALAMEDA et al VS. JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 16, 2007, LINE 13 DEFENDANT SUPERIOR COURT OF ALAMEDA COUNTY'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS SUTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN 10 DAYS AS TO THE BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT CLAIM TO PLEAD CONDUCT THAT DOES NOT SIMPLY RESTATE THE CONTRACT CLAIM. OVERRULED IN ALL OTHER RESPECTS. E.H. MORRILLO V. SATE OF CALIFORNIA (1976) 65 C.2ND 787,794 AUTHORIZES A SUIT BASED ON CONTRACT CLAIMS WHICH IS THE BASIS OF THE ESTOPPEL CLAIM. THE DISCLOSURES IN THE GOVERNMENT CLAIM ARE ADEQUATE UNDER STOCKETT V. ASS'N OF CAL. WATER AGENCIES. (2004) 34 C. 4TH 441,446. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT BASED ON THE FAILURE TO FOLLOW THE PRESCRIBED CONTRACTING METHOD WAS NOT RAISED IN THE MOVING PAPERS AND IS NOT CONSIDERED BY COURT. ATTORNEY'S FEES MAY BE RECOVERED UNDER CCP 1021.5 =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: NANCY BIANCHI VS. JAMES KEHOE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Terms Of Settlement (Ccp Section 664.6); Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 16, 2007, LINE 16 PLAINTIFF NANCY BIANCHI'S Notice Of Motion To Enforce Terms Of Settlement (Ccp Section 664.6); Request For Judicial Notice. Request For Judicial Notice IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. MR. KEHOE'S FAILURE TO LEAVE THE PREMISES ON THE AGREED UPON DATE WAS A MATERIAL BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. IT WAS MR. KEHOE'S PRIMARY OBLIGATION UNDER THE AGREEMENT AND HE CHOSE NOT TO ABIDE BY THAT OBLIGATION. AS A CONSEQUENCE, HE FORFEITED HIS RIGHT TO ANY FURTHER PAYMENT AND HIS TENANCY RIGHTS. =(302/PJM/VC)
